title: The Model State Administrative Procedure Act in the USA
author: Vértesy, László
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper provides a brief but detailed description and analysis which draws attention to the most significant provisions of the Model State Administrative Procedure Act in the United States of America. This Model Act assists the state legislation to adopt and pass legislation governing administrative procedure on a common, federal ground. Therefor each state is granted to modify and supplement the text according to its legal arrangements and demands. This general guidance contains definitions, and rules on rule making, procedure of first instance, remedies, judicial review and relevant administrative offices, officers.
